                        @ondrop="OnDragResetAsync"
                        @ondragleave="OnDragResetAsync"
                        @ondragend="OnDragResetAsync"
                        id="@GetInputId(localIndex)"
+                       @attributes="UserAttributes"
                        required="@Required"
                        aria-required="@Required.ToString().ToLowerInvariant()" />
         }

content.text:
Implemented the fix without committing changes.

Summary:
- Found `MudFileUpload` renders `@attributes="UserAttributes"` before its built-in drag handlers on the internal `InputFile`.
- In Blazor, later duplicate attributes win, so the component’s internal `@ondragleave="OnDragResetAsync"` overrode the user-supplied `@ondragleave` from `UserAttributes`.
- Moved `@attributes="UserAttributes"` after the internal drag handlers in `MudFileUpload.razor`, allowing user drag handlers to take precedence and fire for custom content scenarios.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test proving `@ondragleave` on custom content was not invoked.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix and confirmed the regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary test afterward as requested.
- Ran existing drag-and-drop test:
  - `FileUpload_DragAndDrop_Test` passed.

Final modified file:
- `src/MudBlazor/Components/FileUpload/MudFileUpload.razor`
